title: nirs4all Provider Descriptor v1
$comment: LOCK-PROV / DEC-PROV-001 neutral provider descriptor. This is the LANGUAGE-NEUTRAL projection of a provider client's identity+liveness+capabilities (the Python `ProviderPlugin` {provider_id, version(), health(), capabilities()} is ONE conformant emitter of this shape; an R/JS/WASM provider client must emit the same shape). It carries NO Python type; it is the contract every language client validates against. `writes` mirrors WriteAccess; the read slice never emits `gated`. `executes` is always false for a provider (execution belongs to runtime, see rt_run_request.v1). See docs/contracts/providers/README.md.

schema_version.$comment: The descriptor envelope's own version, independent of any backing package version.

provider_id.$comment: Stable provider identifier. The public provider facets are datasets|repository; benchmarks and papers remain in their owning repositories.

version.$comment: The backing content/service version as reported by the client, or the literal 'unavailable' when the backing is not reachable in this environment.

available.$comment: Whether the backing content/service is reachable for this client (import-available in Python; endpoint/catalogue reachable elsewhere).

reachable.$comment: Optional network-free deeper probe (catalogue enumerable, store/index present). null when no deeper probe is performed.

serves.$comment: Read/serve method names the client exposes. Names are advisory documentation, not a closed vocabulary.

executes.$comment: Always false for a provider. A true value is a contract violation (execution is a runtime concern).

writes.$comment: Write policy. The read slice reaches at most local-cache. `gated` is reserved for a future admin/governance-gated remote write and is never emitted by the read slice.

portability.$comment: Free-text reference to the CAP-002/CAP-004 portability level of served artifacts. References the capability vocabulary; never forks it.
